sn18.8:1.1:
1
… "What do you think, Rahula, is craving for forms … craving for sounds … craving for odours … craving for tastes … craving for tactile objects … craving for mental phenomena permanent or impermanent?" –"Impermanent, venerable sir." …
sn18.8:1.2: "Seeing thus, Rahula, the instructed noble disciple experiences revulsion towards craving for forms … revulsion towards craving for mental phenomena. Experiencing revulsion, he becomes dispassionate … . He understands: ‘… there is no more for this state of being.’"
